common.tagline: Bring your scattered music world back together.
common.disclaimer: An unofficial introduction and user guide. Not affiliated with Apple Inc.
common.appleDisclaimer: Apple, the Apple logo, iPhone, and iPad are trademarks of Apple Inc. App Store is a service mark of Apple Inc.
common.notFound: Sorry, we couldn't find that page.

meta.title: TuneSync — Your music, all in one place
meta.description: Connect your NAS or local folders — TuneSync pulls your scattered songs into one library, fills in artwork and lyrics, and can strip vocals so you can sing along.

home.badge: A native iOS music app built for power users
home.headline: Your music, <accent>all in one place</accent>
home.subhead: Connect your NAS or local folders — TuneSync pulls your scattered songs into one library, fills in artwork and lyrics, and can even strip the vocals so you can sing along.
home.introTitle: Your music shouldn't live across six apps.
home.introBody: TuneSync connects your servers, folders, and player together. Plug in and go.
home.downloadSubtitle: Start on iPhone today and bring your music servers, local library, and player into one place. More platforms are in the works.
home.downloadIOSAria: Open the iOS getting started guide

ios.label: iOS
ios.desc: Built for iPhone — connect a server, organize your library, and play, all here.

android.label: Android
android.desc: Android is on the roadmap, with the same complete music management experience planned.

macos.label: macOS
macos.desc: A desktop experience is coming later, bringing your music world to a larger screen.

windows.label: Windows
windows.desc: Windows is not available yet, but more listening and organizing workflows are planned.

quote1.text: I used to jump between five or six apps to play my own music. Now it's all in one place once I connected the NAS.
quote1.author: Lin Yifan

quote2.text: The AI DJ continuing the queue is genuinely handy — it tops itself up when it runs low, so I don't have to keep picking.
quote2.author: M. Chen

quote3.text: Didn't expect to strip vocals and sing on my phone. Drop the vocal, sing along to the backing, play it back after.
quote3.author: Kai

quote4.text: My library info was a mess. Ran batch organize once and most of the artwork and years got filled in.
quote4.author: Zhang Rui

quote5.text: I like using my own API key — requests go straight to the model I chose, not through anyone else.
quote5.author: D. Wang

quote6.text: It found my server on the LAN automatically. Set it up once and it connected. Easier than I expected.
quote6.author: Eric

home.ctaTitle: Connect your music and start listening.
home.ctaBody: Follow the quick-start guide to link a server — takes a few minutes.

features.title: What it does
features.subtitle: Library, playback, organizing, singing along — all in one app.

library.title: Library
library.desc: Connect WebDAV / SMB / NFS / FTP servers, or import local folders. TuneSync builds one library.
library.featureDesc: On the same network, TuneSync finds your media server automatically — configure once and you're in. Pick which folders to index and that's all it includes. Play as soon as it's done.
library.featureB1: WebDAV / SMB / NFS / FTP / DAAP protocols
library.featureB3: Folder-level indexing — only what you choose
library.featureB4: Mix local folders and remote servers in one library

player.title: Playback
player.desc: Full-screen player with line-by-line synced lyrics, queue, sleep timer, AirPlay.
player.featureTitle: Lyrics highlight as the song plays
player.featureDesc: The player scrolls the lyrics with the song, highlighting the current line. Tap a line to jump there. The background color is pulled from the artwork, and you can switch to plain-text reading mode when there's no timing.

karaoke.title: Karaoke
karaoke.desc: Split vocals and accompaniment on-device, lower the original, record yourself with the mic.
karaoke.featureTitle: Drop the vocals, sing it yourself
karaoke.featureDesc: TuneSync splits a song into vocal and accompaniment tracks right on your phone. Lower the original vocal, sing along, and the mic records what you sing. The first split takes a moment; after that it's cached for next time.
karaoke.featureB2: Live vocal-level control with a 3-2-1 countdown
karaoke.featureB3: Mic recording with input/output latency compensation
karaoke.featureB4: Saves to “My Recordings” for playback and sharing

ai.title: AI assistant
ai.desc: Bring your own API key. Let AI pick the next song or tidy up file names.
ai.featureDesc: Drop in your own API key and it works — OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Zhipu, DeepSeek all have presets, or fill in a custom endpoint or local Ollama. The AI DJ keeps playing based on what you listen to, and can clean up messy file names.
ai.featureB2: Instant / reasoning model roles, auto-selected per task
ai.featureB3: AI DJ with three autonomy levels: preview / one-tap / auto-continue
ai.featureB4: Tool calling: web search, album art, lyrics, playback and playlist control

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.desc: Auto-fill artwork, lyrics, release year, and track numbers.
metadata.featureDesc: TuneSync compares across iTunes, MusicBrainz and other sources to fill in missing artwork, release years, and track numbers. Do it one track at a time or run the whole library. When sources disagree, your weights decide who wins.
metadata.featureB1: iTunes / MusicBrainz / Deezer / AI multi-source matching
metadata.featureB2: Track / album / artist refresh + batch organizing

discovery.title: Discover
discovery.desc: See charts from Apple Music / Bilibili / YouTube, identify songs with ShazamKit.
discovery.featureTitle: When you don't know what to play
discovery.featureDesc: See charts from a few platforms on the home screen. When you're stuck, TuneSync suggests based on what you usually play. Hear a good song out there? Open recognition and it tells you what it is.
discovery.featureB1: Apple Music / Bilibili / YouTube / Last.fm multi-source charts
discovery.featureB2: Suggestions based on what you usually play

intro.title: TuneSync docs
intro.lead: How to use it, page by page.
intro.body: Ordered by what you actually do: connect a server, organize your library, then play, read lyrics, sing along, and finally the AI and metadata stuff. Each page has steps — come back whenever you forget.

gettingStarted.title: Getting started
gettingStarted.lead: First time? Follow the four steps and get your songs in.
gettingStarted.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/getting-started.png

overview.title: The overall flow
overview.body: First launch walks you through four pages — welcome, connect a server, add your library, polish (artwork, lyrics, karaoke) — then drops you into a setup wizard that tracks the rest as a checklist.

step1.title: Welcome
step1.body: A quick intro to what TuneSync does: wires your servers, folders, and player together.

step2.title: Connect servers
step2.body: Supports WebDAV, SMB, NFS, FTP. Connect a server first, then add folders. Manage server status and test connections in one place — once it's set up, you're good.

step3.title: Add your library
step3.body: Add only the folders you want to hear. Once added it plays, and TuneSync fills in artwork and lyrics along the way.

step4.title: Polish
step4.body: Artwork, lyrics, karaoke. Tap a karaoke-ready track to enter sing mode — lyrics scroll on their own, drop the vocal as low as you like, works on the lock screen and in the background.

tip.title: See it again
tip.body: “Settings → User Guide” reopens the tour; “Settings → Setup Guide” keeps a checklist of what's left.

permissions.title: Permissions you'll be asked for
permissions.body: Only when you use the thing that needs it:
permissions.localNetwork: Local network — to find LAN WebDAV / FTP / SMB / NFS servers
permissions.microphone: Microphone — song recognition and karaoke recording
permissions.speech: Speech recognition — transcribes the vocal on-device to add timing to plain lyrics
permissions.applemusic: Apple Music — so playback can continue in the background

next.title: Up next
next.body: Ready to connect a server? Read on in “Library”.

library.title: Library
library.lead: Local folders and remote servers, combined into one library.
library.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/library.png

protocols.title: Supported protocols
protocols.body: Connects over these protocols — self-hosted servers or a NAS:
protocols.webdav: The most common for self-hosting; also supports encrypted WebDAVs
protocols.smb: Windows sharing and most NAS devices, enabled by default
protocols.nfs: Common network filesystem on Linux / NAS
protocols.ftp: Traditional file transfer; supports encrypted FTPS

step1.title: Open library sources
step1.body: Under “Settings → Library Sources & Indexing”, tap “Add Server”, or go in from the Home “Connect a Server” prompt.

step2.title: Pick a protocol, enter the address
step2.body: Choose WebDAV / SMB / NFS / FTP, then the address, port, and share path. Not sure it's reachable? Hit “Test Connection” first.

step3.title: Enter credentials, save
step3.body: Add username and password if it needs them. After saving, the server shows up in the list as connected.

step4.title: Pick folders to index
step4.body: Browse the server and check only the folders you want. Plays as soon as it's done.

discover.title: Bonjour auto-discovery
discover.body: On the same network, TuneSync finds _webdav._tcp, _smb._tcp, _ftp._tcp, _nfs._tcp on its own and drops you straight into setup — no typing addresses.

indexing.title: Indexing
indexing.body: Once folders are added, TuneSync scans the audio, reads embedded info, and builds the library. Plays when it's done. It runs in the background; see progress on Home and under “Settings → Background Tasks & Logs”.
indexing.note: Only the folders you pick get indexed — it won't pull in the whole disk.

playlists.title: Playlists and favorites
playlists.favorite: Favorite a track — favorites show up on Home and in the favorites list

player.title: Player
player.lead: Full-screen, mini, queue, and the usual playback controls.
player.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/player-lyrics.png

modes.title: Three modes
modes.body: Switch between three at the bottom:
modes.normal: Standard: big artwork, track info, scrubber, controls

menu.title: Top menu
menu.body: The full-screen player's top menu has:

queue.title: Play queue
queue.body: The queue is what's coming up — tap to play, swipe to remove, or clear all (it asks again first). When it's empty it nudges you to add from the library.

bg.title: Background and lock screen
bg.body: Keeps playing on the lock screen and in the background. Control Center and the lock screen both control it.

lyrics.title: Lyrics
lyrics.lead: Line-by-line synced lyrics, plain reading mode, and several lyrics sources.
lyrics.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/lyrics.png

modes.title: Two modes
modes.body: Switches automatically based on whether there's timing:
modes.synced: Synced: aligned to playback, current line highlighted, auto-scroll, tap a line to jump, drag to browse — it resumes following when you let go
modes.reading: Reading: just plain-text lyrics, no timing

sources.title: Where lyrics come from
sources.lrclib: Open lyrics DB — exact match first, then a fuzzy search
sources.genius: Found by search, then parsed from the page

webdav.v: a .lrc / .txt next to the audio, pulled over WebDAV

import.title: Import LRC
import.body: From the player's top menu, pick “Import LRC” and choose a file. It reads the timing and applies right away.

generate.title: Generate synced lyrics
generate.body: Only have plain text? Pick “Generate Synced Lyrics” from the top menu. TuneSync listens for when the vocal happens on-device, then lines it up with the text — and tells you how confident it is along the way.

confidence.title: About “confidence”
confidence.body: AI-aligned lyrics come with a confidence score. If it's low it warns you and you can retry. The result is stored on the track so it's not recomputed.

karaoke.title: Karaoke
karaoke.lead: Split vocals and accompaniment on-device, lower the original, record yourself with the mic.
karaoke.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/karaoke.png

how.title: How it works
how.body: In karaoke mode, TuneSync splits the song into “vocal” and “accompaniment” tracks right on your phone, and the two volumes are independent: drop the vocal, keep the accompaniment, sing along.

step1.title: Prepare the split
step1.body: In the player, tap the mic button and pick “Prepare Karaoke”. TuneSync splits the song into vocal and accompaniment on-device, processes it in chunks, and caches the result for next time.

step2.title: Start
step2.body: When it's ready the button becomes “Start Karaoke”. Tap it — the accompaniment plays, vocal dropped.

step3.title: Adjust the vocal, record
step3.body: Use the vertical slider to set how much vocal stays. Want to record? Tap record, count down 3-2-1, and it captures your voice mixed with the accompaniment.

step4.title: Save, play back
step4.body: On stop you can “Save and Stop”, “Discard”, or “Keep going”. Saved recordings live in “Settings → My Recordings” — play, share, or clear them.

balance.title: Vocal slider
balance.body: The karaoke console has a vertical slider: push up to raise the original vocal, push to the top to collapse it back into the mic button. After the split finishes it pops open with a little haptic.

record.title: Recording
record.meter: Live input level (green → yellow → red) and a timer while recording
record.pause: Pause and resume, in sync with playback
record.save: On stop: “Save and Stop”, “Discard”, or “Keep going”

model.title: First time needs a model
model.body: Vocal separation uses an on-device model (MDX-light). First use means getting it ready under “Settings → Vocal Separation”; it works in chunks stitched together, caches the result for reuse — the first run is a bit slower.

recordings.title: My Recordings
recordings.body: Saved recordings are in “Settings → My Recordings” — play, share, or clear to free space.

live.title: Live Activity
live.body: Separation supports Live Activity — progress shows in the Dynamic Island and on the lock screen.

ai.title: AI assistant
ai.lead: Bring your own API key. Let AI pick songs, tidy file names, or write album intros.
ai.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/ai-dj.png

step1.title: Turn it on
step1.body: Under “Settings → Metadata & Lyrics → AI Assistance”, flip on “AI Assistance”.

step2.title: Pick a provider
step2.body: Under “Connection” choose one — OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Zhipu, DeepSeek and others are preset, or enter a custom endpoint — then the Base URL.

step3.title: Enter key and model
step3.body: Paste your API key, then pick a fast model and a reasoning model (type a name or choose from “Available Models”).

step4.title: Test it
step4.body: Tap “Test Connection” to see if it works, or send a message in “AI Trial Chat” — if it replies, you're set.

roles.title: Two models
roles.body: Two roles, picked automatically by task:

instant.v: Quick and cheap — recommendations, filename cleanup, that kind of thing

reasoning.v: Slower, higher quality — writes album intros; you can set how hard it thinks

dj.title: AI DJ
dj.body: The DJ queues songs based on what you usually play, with three levels:
dj.auto: Tops up the queue itself when it's running low

tools.title: Tool calling
tools.body: When on, the model can use these tools (some need your OK):

privacy.title: On privacy
privacy.body: It uses your own key and sends requests straight to your chosen provider. Turn on tool calling and “smart tool selection” as needed — keep the capability, control the cost.

trial.title: Trial chat
trial.body: There's a trial chat under “AI Assistance” to check the connection without opening a specific feature.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.lead: Compared across sources, fills in artwork, lyrics, and track info so the library stops being a mess.
metadata.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/metadata.png

sources.title: Sources
sources.body: TuneSync pulls from these and picks the best match:
sources.itunes: Artwork, release info, album tracklist; artwork up to 1200×1200
sources.ai: AI tidies file names and guesses missing fields

embedded.v: tags already in the audio file

single.title: Refresh one track
single.body: From track details or the player's top menu, pick “Search Metadata”. It lists candidates scored by match quality — pick one and it writes back.

album.title: A whole album
album.body: The album page can refresh the whole thing:
album.intro: Have AI write an album intro (checks the web first, streams as it writes)

artist.title: A single artist
artist.body: The artist page refreshes the artist — bio, image, MusicBrainz / Wikidata / Wikipedia IDs — and applies it to all that artist's tracks.

weights.title: Source weights
weights.body: Under “Settings → Metadata & Lyrics” set weights and per-field “overwrite” rules — who wins when sources disagree, whether to touch what's already there.

batch.title: Batch organize
batch.body: Whole-library batch refresh works too; the background task center tracks progress. Pick a strategy: “standard”, “metadata only”, “batch disk artwork”.

history.title: History and rollback
history.body: Every refresh records who gave each field and what it changed to — handy for looking back or reverting.

discovery.title: Discover
discovery.lead: Home recommendations, multi-platform charts, and song recognition to find your next track.
discovery.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/discovery.png

home.title: Home
home.body: Home lays out sections based on your library:
home.foryou: For You: a recommendation playlist based on your taste

trends.title: Multi-platform charts
trends.body: After enabling chart sources under “Settings → Home & Discovery”, Home shows them:

foryou.title: For You
foryou.body: For You puts out a main pick (a collage of a few tracks' artwork) plus secondary picks, each with a reason and a “Preview Mix” — tap to preview.

feedback.title: Feedback tuning
feedback.body: Not loving a pick? Give the AI DJ feedback (like / less of this) from the player menu and it adjusts.

recognize.title: Recognition
recognize.body: Identify what's playing around you with ShazamKit. History is kept — browse it under “Settings → Song Recognition”.

settings.title: Settings
settings.lead: Appearance, language, player animation, cache, background tasks.
settings.shotHint: Replace with a real screenshot: public/screenshots/settings.png

cache.title: Cache and storage
cache.body: Under “Settings → Cache & Storage”, see how much artwork, audio, and temp cache take up — and clear them.

tasks.title: Background tasks and logs
tasks.body: “Settings → Background Tasks & Logs” puts progress, cancellable tasks, and failure reasons for four task types in one place:

guide.title: User guide
guide.body: “Settings → User Guide” reopens the first-launch tour; “Settings → Setup Guide” keeps a checklist of what's left.

about.title: About
about.body: “Settings → About TuneSync” — version, whether the separation model is ready, third-party credits.
